questions.q: What work did young Satyavati do on the river?
options: She sold fish in the market of Hastinapura
She wove nets for the fishermen's guild
She ferried travelers across the water in a boat
She gathered pearls for the king of Chedi
questions.explanation: Raised among fisherfolk, Satyavati plied a ferry boat, which is how the sage Parashara came to meet her.

questions.q: What was Satyavati called in her youth because she smelled of fish?
options: Matsyagandha
Padmagandha
Kalindi
Sugandha
questions.explanation: Born of a fish, she carried its odor and was known as Matsyagandha, the fish-scented one, until Parashara transformed her.

questions.q: Which sage gave Satyavati her divine fragrance?
options: Vasishtha
Bharadwaja
Durvasa
Parashara
questions.explanation: The sage Parashara, struck by her beauty on the ferry, replaced her fishy odor with a heavenly fragrance.

questions.q: Who was Satyavati's secret son, born before her royal marriage?
options: Vidura
Vyasa
Shuka
Sanjaya
questions.explanation: Her union with Parashara produced Krishna Dvaipayana Vyasa, the future composer of the Mahabharata itself.

questions.q: Which king did Satyavati marry?
options: Pratipa of Hastinapura
Uparichara of Chedi
Shantanu of Hastinapura
Shalva of Saubha
questions.explanation: King Shantanu of Hastinapura fell in love with her by the river and married her after Devavrata's great oath cleared the way.

questions.q: Who raised Satyavati as his daughter?
options: A chief of fishermen who worked the river
A forest sage in his hermitage
The royal cowherd of Hastinapura
A merchant on the banks of the Ganga
questions.explanation: The fisher chief, often called Dasharaja, found and raised Satyavati, and later set the famous condition on her marriage.

questions.q: From what creature was Satyavati born?
options: A swan on the sacred lake
A fish swimming in the river
A doe in the deep forest
A serpent of the lower worlds
questions.explanation: Satyavati was born from a fish that had swallowed a king's seed, which is why she first smelled of fish.

questions.q: What were Satyavati's two sons by her royal husband named?
options: Dhritarashtra and Pandu
Devavrata and Devapi
Bahlika and Somadatta
Chitrangada and Vichitravirya
questions.explanation: Satyavati bore Shantanu two sons, Chitrangada and Vichitravirya, both of whom died without leaving heirs.

questions.q: What condition secured Satyavati's marriage to the king?
options: That her sons, not the crown prince, would inherit the throne
That she be crowned before the wedding
That the king renounce all other wives
That her father be made chief minister
questions.explanation: Her foster father demanded that Satyavati's sons inherit Hastinapura, a condition met only by Devavrata's terrible oath.

questions.q: Who took a terrible vow so that Satyavati could become queen?
options: Vidura
Chitrangada
Devavrata, later called Bhishma
Vichitravirya
questions.explanation: Devavrata renounced the throne and swore celibacy to satisfy her father's condition, earning the name Bhishma.

questions.q: What name did Satyavati gain when her new fragrance carried a full yojana?
options: Matsyagandha
Yojanagandha
Padmavati
Adrika
questions.explanation: Because her divine scent could be perceived from a yojana away, she became known as Yojanagandha.

questions.q: Besides fragrance, what else did Parashara grant Satyavati after their union?
options: A son who would never know sorrow
A promise that she would one day be queen
Protection from all curses for her descendants
The restoration of her maidenhood
questions.explanation: Parashara granted that her virginity be restored, so the secret birth of Vyasa left no mark on her future.

questions.q: Where was Satyavati's first son born, earning him the name Dvaipayana?
options: On an island in the river Yamuna
In a hermitage in the Himalayan foothills
In the fisher village by the Ganga
In the palace gardens of Chedi
questions.explanation: Vyasa was born on an island in the Yamuna, and Dvaipayana means the island-born.

questions.q: What parting promise did her newborn son Vyasa make to Satyavati?
options: He would return every year at the monsoon
He would compose an epic in her honor
He would appear whenever she thought of him
He would guard her sons from all harm
questions.explanation: Vyasa, who grew to manhood at birth, departed for austerities promising to come to his mother the moment she remembered him.

questions.q: When her royal line stood heirless, whom did Satyavati first urge to father children on the widowed queens?
options: Her own son Vyasa
A worthy brahmin of the court
The king of a friendly realm
Bhishma, despite his vow
questions.explanation: Satyavati first begged Bhishma to marry the widows or sire heirs, and only after his refusal did she reveal Vyasa's existence.

questions.q: After Bhishma refused, whom did Satyavati summon to save the dynasty?
options: The sage Parashara
Her firstborn son Vyasa
Her brother from the fisher clan
The family priest of the Kurus
questions.explanation: Remembering Vyasa's promise, Satyavati summoned him to sire heirs on Vichitravirya's widows by niyoga.

questions.q: Which three grandsons were born of the union Satyavati arranged through Vyasa?
options: Dhritarashtra, Pandu, and Vidura
Chitrangada, Vichitravirya, and Devavrata
Yudhishthira, Bhima, and Arjuna
Karna, Drona, and Kripa
questions.explanation: Vyasa fathered Dhritarashtra on Ambika, Pandu on Ambalika, and Vidura on a serving maid, rescuing the Kuru line.

questions.q: How did Satyavati's elder son Chitrangada die?
options: Of a wasting disease in his youth
Drowned crossing the swollen Ganga
Slain in battle by a gandharva who bore his own name
Poisoned by a rival claimant to the throne
questions.explanation: A gandharva also named Chitrangada challenged the proud young king and killed him after a long combat.

questions.q: What fate took Satyavati's younger son Vichitravirya?
options: He fell in battle against the Panchalas
He died young of disease, leaving no children
He renounced the throne and vanished into the forest
He was slain by a curse for insulting a sage
questions.explanation: Vichitravirya died childless of disease, commonly described as consumption, leaving the dynasty without an heir.

questions.q: Why was Satyavati's grandson Dhritarashtra born blind?
options: His mother Ambika shut her eyes before Vyasa
Vyasa cursed the child for his mother's pride
A sage's curse on the Kuru line claimed him
Satyavati's own broken promise demanded a price
questions.explanation: Frightened by Vyasa's grim appearance, Ambika closed her eyes during their union, so her son was born blind.

questions.q: Why was her grandson Pandu born pale?
options: He was born under the waning moon
Ganga's chill waters touched his mother
A blessing of Surya marked him with light
His mother Ambalika turned white with fear before Vyasa
questions.explanation: Ambalika went pale at the sight of Vyasa, and her son Pandu was therefore born with a pale complexion.

questions.q: Who was sent to Vyasa in the queens' place at the third summons?
options: A princess of a vassal house
Satyavati's own younger sister
A serving maid, who bore the wise Vidura
A daughter of the family priest
questions.explanation: Ambika sent her serving maid instead of going herself, and the maid received Vyasa calmly and bore the wise Vidura.

questions.q: Which celestial nymph, cursed to live as a fish, gave birth to Satyavati?
options: Adrika
Menaka
Urvashi
Ghritachi
questions.explanation: The apsara Adrika, cursed to live as a fish in the Yamuna, swallowed a king's seed and bore Satyavati and her twin.

questions.q: Whose seed, swallowed by the fish, fathered Satyavati and her twin?
options: King Pratipa of Hastinapura
The sage Parashara
King Uparichara Vasu of Chedi
The river god of the Yamuna
questions.explanation: King Uparichara Vasu of Chedi was the father, his seed having been carried to the river and swallowed by the cursed fish.

questions.q: Who was Satyavati's twin, born from the same fish?
options: A serpent prince raised in the depths
A boy named Matsya, taken by the king
A daughter named Kali, raised in Chedi
A boy named Dasharaja, the fisher chief
questions.explanation: The fish yielded twins; the boy Matsya was kept by King Uparichara, while the girl was given to the fisher chief to raise.

questions.q: On whose counsel did Satyavati finally leave the palace for the forest?
options: Bhishma's, after the burning of the lac house
Vidura's, when the dice game was announced
Kunti's, after the death of Madri
Vyasa's, who warned that joyless times were coming
questions.explanation: After Pandu's death, Vyasa told his mother the happy days were over and dark times ahead, so she retired to the forest with Ambika and Ambalika.

questions.q: What waiting period did Vyasa request before the niyoga, which Satyavati's urgency overruled?
options: A year of vows so the queens could bear worthy sons
A month of fasting for the whole court
Six years until the widows finished mourning
A single night of prayer at the Ganga
questions.explanation: Vyasa asked that the queens observe a year-long vow to purify themselves, but Satyavati insisted the kingdom could not wait.

questions.q: How did Parashara hide his union with Satyavati from watching eyes?
options: He stopped time along the river's course
He took her to an underwater palace
He made them both invisible to mortals
He wrapped the boat in a thick screen of mist
questions.explanation: Parashara created a dense fog around the boat on the river, concealing them from both banks.

questions.q: By what name was the dark-complexioned Satyavati also known?
options: Krishnaa
Shyama
Kali
Tamasi
questions.explanation: Because of her dark complexion Satyavati was also called Kali, while Krishnaa is the parallel name borne by Draupadi.

questions.q: What became of the apsara Adrika once the twins were taken from her fish body?
options: She remained a fish guarding the Yamuna
Freed from her curse, she returned to the heavens
She became a river flowing through Chedi
She served Satyavati as guardian spirit
questions.explanation: Bearing the two human children fulfilled the terms of Adrika's curse, and she regained her celestial form and rose to heaven.
